mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in aggregate demand signals an expansionary monetary policy that boosts economic growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in labor productivity signals a contractionary fiscal policy that reduces aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in contraction periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary policy that reduces economic growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in labor market participation signals a recession that reduces aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in recession periods are significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
